div.p: David king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, French, English, and Galwegian, sends greeting. Know me to have given and granted to Robert de Brus in feu and heritage, to him and his heir in forest, Annandale on both sides of the Water of Annan, just as the boundaries are from the forest of Selkirk as far as his land extends towards Nithsdale and towards the Clyde, freely and quitly just as he holds his other forest well and freely. Wherefore I forbid that anyone may hunt in the aforesaid forest unless with his permission, on pain of the forefieture of ten pounds, and that anyone may go through the aforesaid forest unless by the proper, named ways. As witness: Walter the chancellor; and Hugh de Morville; and Walter son of Alan; and Odenel d'Umfraville; and Walter of Lindsay; and Richard de Moreville. At Staplegordon.
